title: Internet / DNS not working when using custom network
body:
### Issue Description

When I run a container with the default network, dns works just fine:
```
> podman run --rm -it alpine nslookup google.com
Server:         172.29.64.1
Address:        172.29.64.1:53

Non-authoritative answer:
Name:   google.com
Address: 142.250.186.174

Non-authoritative answer:
Name:   google.com
Address: 2a00:1450:4001:802::200e
```

However, as soon as I use a custom network, dns fails on me:
```
> podman network create --driver bridge test
> podman run --rm --network test -it alpine nslookup google.com
;; connection timed out; no servers could be reached
```

A container created with the default network has this in `/etc/resolv.conf`:
```
nameserver 10.0.2.3
nameserver 172.29.64.1
```

When I create a container with a custom network instead, it looks like this:
```
search dns.podman
nameserver 10.89.1.1
```

If I now enter the container and replace nameserver with eg. `8.8.8.8` everything works again.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `podman network create --driver bridge test`
2. `podman run --rm --network test -it alpine nslookup google.com`

### Describe the results you received

No dns / internet when using a custom network

### Describe the results you expected

Working dns resolution
